title: Charred Dense Spanish Coconut Brunch
description: A rich and savory, charred, Spanish-inspired brunch dish featuring coconut cream and a miso-marinated egg. Serve in a mason jar for a rustic touch.
instructions: In a small bowl, whisk together the miso paste, smoked paprika, garlic powder, salt, and black pepper for the miso-marinate.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the coconut cream, lemon juice, and a pinch of salt for the coconut cream topping.
Warm a small amount of coconut oil in the microwave or a saucepan for the mason jar cooking process.
Place the marrow bones in a small baking dish and roast in the oven for 10 minutes.
In a small ramekin, submerge the egg in the miso-marinate mixture, ensuring the egg is fully coated.
Remove the baked marrow bones from the oven and carefully crack the marinated egg into the marrow holes.
Transfer the baking dish to the oven and cook for an additional 8-10 minutes or until the whites are set and the yolks are cooked to desired doneness.
While the egg cooks, toast some fresh cilantro in a small skillet with a bit of olive oil for garnish.
After the egg is cooked, remove it from the oven and top with the coconut cream topping, some toasted cilantro, and a sprinkle of salt and pepper.
Serve the charred dense Spanish coconut brunch in a mason jar with the toasted coconut cream and cilantro on top.
Refrigerate any leftovers for up to 3 days and store in an airtight container.
